deal_summary: Bristol Myers Squibb and SystImmune are collaborating on the development of BL-B01D1, a bispecific antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targeting EGFR and HER3 for breast cancer. The article reports on promising Phase 1b trial results in China, showing the drug's efficacy in heavily pretreated patients with metastatic breast cancer. This appears to be part of an ongoing partnership between the companies focused on advancing this ADC candidate.

Eli Lilly announced a partnership with NVIDIA to deploy a supercomputer for drug discovery and development, focusing on escaping traditional pharma lifecycle constraints through advanced computing capabilities.
